title: 10 best herbal teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy
intro_desc: Herbal teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy are a natural alternative remedy that helps alleviate symptoms associated with enlarged prostate. These teas contain bioactive compounds that inhibit the growth of prostate cells, reduce inflammation, and improve urinary function. Examples include saw palmetto tea, pygeum bark tea, and stinging nettle tea. Drinking these teas regularly can help reduce frequent urination, improve overall bladder control, and even alleviate symptoms such as incontinence and painful urination. This natural approach can significantly improve the quality of life for men suffering from benign prostatic hypertrophy, allowing them to live with greater comfort and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Puncture vine teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as urinary frequency and hesitancy. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling in the prostate gland, making it easier for urine to flow through the urethra. Additionally, puncture vine teas contain antioxidants that may help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to prostatic tissue.
remedy_properties: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of BPH such as enlarged prostate and urinary frequency.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce prostatic tissue proliferation and improve urine flow in individuals with BPH.

Beta-amyrin: As a natural 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor, beta-amyrin can help block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is thought to contribute to the growth of enlarged prostate tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the puncture vine plant contain alkaloids and saponins, which help to relax the prostate muscles and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the puncture vine plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried tribulus terrestris roots and stems from a reputable source.
Rinse the dried herbs with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine the rinsed tribulus terrestris with 500ml of boiling water in a medium-sized teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains fatty acids that inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement. As DHT levels decrease, the prostate gland begins to shrink, alleviating symptoms such as frequent urination and weak stream. Additionally, saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce prostate swelling and ease discomfort associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy, making it a popular natural remedy for men seeking relief from these common symptoms.
remedy_properties: Inhibits 5-alpha-reductase enzyme: Saw palmetto tea's fatty acid content inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH, thereby reducing prostate size and symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate gland, which can contribute to BPH development and progression.
Anti-androgenic effects: Saw palmetto tea has been shown to block the binding of testosterone to its receptors, which may help slow down or stop the growth of benign prostatic tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used in herbal remedies as they contain high levels of fatty acids and phytosterols, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of BPH by reducing inflammation and blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT).

Fruits: The berries or fruits of the saw palmetto plant are used in teas due to their rich content of beta-sitosterol, a compound that has been found to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme involved in the conversion of testosterone to DHT.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried serenoa repens leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a saucepan or teapot for six to eight minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or glass bottle.
Combine the strained tea with one tablespoon of honey and one lemon slice as desired.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African plum teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its unique blend of phytochemicals and flavonoids. The tea's primary active compound, punicalagins, has been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells, reducing inflammation and swelling in the prostate gland. Additionally, the tea's high concentration of antioxidants helps to combat oxidative stress and DNA damage, which can contribute to BPH development. By addressing these underlying causes, African plum teas may help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination and urinary retention associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: African Plum Tea contains a range of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate gland, potentially slowing down BPH progression.

Alpha-blocker properties: The tea's flavonoids, particularly epicatechin, exhibit alpha-blocker activity, which can help relax smooth muscle cells in the prostate, reducing symptoms of BPH such as urinary frequency and urgency.

5-alpha-reductase inhibitory effects: African Plum Tea's saponins have been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase enzyme, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement in BPH. By blocking this conversion, the tea may help slow down prostate growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks contain antioxidants and flavonoids that work to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, providing relief from prostate issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried prunus africana bark from your local health food store or online supplier.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Boil one cup of water in a saucepan and add one teaspoon of ground prunus africana bark for three to five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids within one minute.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional five minutes before serving warm or at room temperature, drinking once daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains a compound called arbutin, which is converted into hydroquinone in the body. Hydroquinone has been shown to have a potent anti-inflammatory effect on the prostate gland, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with BPH. Additionally, bearberry tea has natural diuretic properties, which help to flush out excess water and reduce pressure on the urinary tract, further alleviating symptoms of BPH. Regular consumption of bearberry tea may provide relief from frequent urination, nocturia, and other common symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_properties: Urinary Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Bearberry tea's urushiolic acid and arbutin contents have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland and alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, such as urinary frequency and urgency.

Alpha-Adrenergic Blocking Activity: The plant's constituents, particularly arbutoside A, exhibit alpha-adrenergic blocking properties, which can relax smooth muscle tissue in the prostate, bladder neck, and urethra, reducing symptoms of BPH like urinary retention and frequent urination.

Antioxidant Activity: Bearberry tea's high antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress and DNA damage in the prostate gland, potentially slowing down the progression of BPH and its related complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bearberry plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve urine flow, making them an effective component in teas for treating BPH.
Fruits: Bearberry fruits are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the prostate gland.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems from a trusted supplier.
Measure two teaspoons of the dried herb into a clean glass tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the herbal mixture, allowing it to steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large ceramic mug, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, ideally sipping warm during the morning and evening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains natural compounds that help to relax the muscles in the prostate gland, reducing inflammation and swelling. The antioxidants present in herbal tea also help to reduce oxidative stress and improve overall urinary health. Additionally, certain herbs such as saw palmetto and stinging nettle have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement. By reducing inflammation and DHT levels, herbal tea may help alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto: Inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH, reducing symptoms such as enlarged prostate and urinary frequency.
Pygeum Africanum: Reduces inflammation in the prostate gland, which can alleviate symptoms like painful urination and frequent nighttime awakenings due to urinary issues.
Uva Ursi: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, helping to soothe and calm irritated bladder and urinary tract tissues, alleviating BPH symptoms like burning during urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea plant contain flavonoids and polyphenols that help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH).
Bark: The bark of the tea plant has been traditionally used to treat prostate issues due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve urine flow.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves and pour boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five minutes before straining and discarding solids.
Combine equal parts of camellia sinensis and pygeum africanum bark, steeping for seven minutes.
Blend one cup of cooled camellia sinensis tea with one quarter cup of chopped stinging nettle leaves.
Store the final blend in an airtight container for up to three days and consume hot or iced.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tomato teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain high levels of lycopene, a powerful antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the prostate gland. The anti-inflammatory properties of lycopene help to alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency and nocturia associated with BPH. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids and carotenoids work together to improve blood flow to the prostate, reducing swelling and discomfort caused by enlarged tissue.
remedy_properties: Saw palmetto tea: May help reduce symptoms by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which can contribute to prostate enlargement.
Pygeum africanum tea: May help reduce inflammation and improve urinary symptoms by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which can cause prostate swelling.
Horsetail tea: May help support prostate health by providing antioxidants and other nutrients that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tomato plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of BPH.
Fruits: Tomatoes have been shown to have a positive effect on prostate health due to their high concentration of lycopene, an antioxidant that can help prevent or slow down BPH.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh solanum lycopersicum leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free of pesticides and heavy metals.
Rinse the harvested plant material thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried solanum lycopersicum leaves and flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a medium-sized pot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Drink the resulting tea three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, for a minimum of six weeks to alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zucchini teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its rich antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the prostate gland. The herbal tea's anti-inflammatory compounds work to soothe the enlarged prostate tissue, alleviating symptoms such as frequent urination, weak stream, and difficulty starting or stopping flow. Additionally, the tea's diuretic properties help to increase urine production, further reducing pressure on the bladder and prostate gland.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto: Saw palmetto may help reduce symptoms of BPH by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which can contribute to prostate enlargement.

Stinging Nettle: Stinging nettle tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, which may help reduce inflammation and slow down the growth of prostate cells in BPH patients.
Soy Isoflavones: Soy isoflavones, such as genistein, may help alleviate BPH symptoms by reducing DHT levels and inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in prostate cell proliferation.

Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, barks help to alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cucurbita pepo leaves or dried cucurbita pepo powder and measure 2 teaspoons of the herb into a heat-resistant container.
Boil one cup of pure water in a saucepan and pour it over the cucurbita pepo, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well to dissolve, using one tablespoon of honey per cup of tea if desired.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption, drinking one to two cups daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with enlarged prostate. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can ease urinary tract discomfort, frequency, and urgency, while also improving overall urinary flow. Additionally, stinging nettle tea may help to shrink the prostate gland by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to prostatic growth. This natural remedy provides a gentle, yet effective way to manage benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Stinging nettle tea contains a high amount of antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate gland, potentially alleviating symptoms of BPH.
Alpha-linolenic acid (ALA): Nettle tea is rich in ALA, an omega-3 fatty acid that has been shown to inhibit the growth of abnormal prostate cells and may help reduce the size of enlarged prostates.

Phytochemicals: Stinging nettle contains a variety of phytochemicals, including flavonoids and lignans, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-androgenic properties that may help regulate hormone levels and reduce inflammation in the prostate gland.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of lignans, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their rich content of fatty acids and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve urinary function in individuals with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine one tablespoon of dried nettle leaves with one cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat three times daily, one hour before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the prostate gland. The tea's diuretic properties also help to increase urine production, alleviating symptoms of enlarged prostate such as frequent urination and nocturia. Additionally, roselle tea has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that contribute to prostate hypertrophy, making it a natural and effective remedy for managing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Roselle tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate, which can contribute to BPH symptoms.

Alpha-blocking compounds: The alpha-blocking compounds present in roselle tea, such as punicalagins and ellagic acid, have been shown to relax smooth muscle in the prostate, reducing symptoms of BPH like urinary frequency and urgency.

Androgen receptor blockers: Roselle tea's flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have been found to inhibit the activity of androgens (male hormones) that can contribute to the growth of prostatic tissue and lead to BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the roots are known to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks contain anthocyanins and flavones that have been traditionally used to treat prostate issues by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh hibiscus sabdariffa flowers or use 4 teaspoons of dried flowers for steeping.
Boil one cup of water in a tea kettle and pour it over the hibiscus flowers in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flowers to infuse into the hot water and release their active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Drink the hibiscus tea 3 times a day for a period of 6 weeks to experience potential benefits in alleviating bph symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work together to alleviate symptoms. The tea's natural compounds, such as beta-sitosterol and other phytosterols, have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone believed to contribute to enlarged prostate growth. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the herbs help to reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, further relieving symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy, such as frequent urination and difficulty starting or stopping the flow of urine.
remedy_properties: Caffeine: Caffeine in herbal corn tea may help reduce BPH symptoms by relaxing the prostate muscles, thereby decreasing pressure on the urethra and improving urinary flow.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal corn tea may help protect the prostate gland from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to BPH development and progression.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin, found in some herbal corn teas, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which is a common characteristic of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of BPH.

Rhyzomes: Contains a unique combination of alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-androgenic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce prostate size and alleviate urinary tract issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and wash fresh corn silk from mature ears of zea mays.
Dry the corn silk in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Steep one teaspoon of dried corn silk in boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool for three minutes before drinking and enjoy.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy with herbal teas is by combining a blend of soothing and diuretic herbs. A mixture of saw palmetto, pygeum, and stinging nettle tea has been shown to reduce symptoms such as frequent urination and nocturia. Drinking 2-3 cups per day may help alleviate BPH symptoms without the risk of side effects associated with pharmaceutical treatments. Additionally, goldenrod and horsetail teas can also be added to support urinary health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to benign prostatic hypertrophy that are treated with herbal teas are those affecting urinary health and prostate function. Herbal teas like saw palmetto, pygeum, and stinging nettle have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of enlarged prostate, such as frequent urination and difficulty starting a stream. These teas may also help relieve the symptoms of overactive bladder, interstitial cystitis, and prostatitis by reducing inflammation and promoting urinary tract relaxation.
